query:
Q1: CycleReviewRecord location — new module             

  New cycle_review_index.rs module. SqlxStore is already doing too much;    
  CYCLE_REVIEW_INDEX is a distinct concern (keyed archive ops, not entry
  CRUD). The module boundary makes the separation explicit and keeps        
  SqlxStore under 500 lines. Researcher is right.                 

  Q2: CURRENT_DETECTION_RULES_VERSION — neither                             
  
  Don't import from unimatrix-observe and don't hardcode in the handler.    
  Define a single SUMMARY_SCHEMA_VERSION: u32 const in cycle_review_index.rs
   and bump it when either the detection rules or the JSON structure        
  changes. The two versioning concerns (rules version, serialization format)
   should be unified into one number — dual-versioning adds complexity with
  no benefit at this scale. Keep the const local; no cross-crate coupling
  for an integer.

  Q3: RetrospectiveReport serde completeness

  Not a design decision — compile-time gate item. Add to AC:                
  "RetrospectiveReport or a dedicated DTO is fully Serialize + Deserialize
  (compile verification at delivery)." If any field is non-serializable, a  
  separate serializable DTO is required. Don't block scope on this — catch
  it in implementation.

  Q4: Stale-detection policy — always return stored record                  
  
  The spec's implied behavior is correct. Silent recompute breaks           
  idempotency — same call, different time, different result depending on
  invisible rules version state. Surface schema_version in the response so  
  callers can see it and decide whether force=true is warranted. Something
  like: "computed with schema_version 1, current is 2 — use force=true to
  recompute." The caller drives recompute explicitly; the tool never
  surprises them.

  Q5: pending_cycle_reviews scope — cycle_events only, within K-window

  Old observation_metrics-based cycles predate the entire CYCLE_REVIEW_INDEX
   design. Including them floods the list with history that can't be
  remediated and isn't subject to #409's purge. pending_cycle_reviews has a 
  specific operational purpose: "what to run retro on before signals
  expire." Scope it to cycles within the K-window that have query_log rows
  with feature_cycle set but no CYCLE_REVIEW_INDEX entry. No
  pre-cycle_events archaeology.

  Q6: context_status perf — always compute

  The query is a set difference between two small tables — cheap. Gate it   
  and it disappears from the normal status view, which defeats the purpose.
  Pending reviews are a health signal; they belong in the always-on report. 
  Constrain the query to the K-window (matching Q5) so it stays fast as
  query_log grows, and it's fine unconditionally.\
Revise scope to align

entries.title: ADR-002 crt-033: Unified SUMMARY_SCHEMA_VERSION const in cycle_review_index.rs
Keyed-archive module pattern for unimatrix-store (memoization/index tables)
crt-033 memoization: check_stored_review returns Result, not Option — callers handle cache-miss via Err branch
ADR-004 crt-025: Phase Narrative as Optional Field on RetrospectiveReport
ADR: Server-Store Decoupling Evaluated and Rejected
